title: Thelypteris neohibernica
intro: Thelypteris neohibernica, also known as New Zealand Lady Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris neohibernica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Thelypteris neohibernica is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Thelypteris neohibernica is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of vascular plants that are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. The Thelypteridaceae family is a part of the larger group known as the Polypodiales, an order of ferns that includes over 8,000 species. Thelypteris neohibernica is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within this family. Its classification as a species of fern is further supported by its characteristics, such as its pinnate leaves and rhizomatous growth habit.
